name: chart-line
title: ChartLine
description: Static single- or multi-series line chart for trends across ordered, evenly spaced categories, including preformatted time-period labels.

intent.summary: Static single- or multi-series line chart for trends across ordered, evenly spaced categories, including preformatted time-period labels.
useWhen: Users need to follow one or more numeric trends across a shared ordered x-axis.
Time periods are already grouped into equally spaced labels such as months, weeks, or days.
A horizontal ChartLegend should identify multiple series.
avoidWhen: Pixel position must reflect irregular elapsed time or continuous timestamps.
The primary task is comparing unrelated category magnitudes rather than following a trend.
The chart needs built-in zoom, brushing, selection, or drill-down.
Series lengths do not align with the shared category list.

alternatives.when: Users need to compare one measure across discrete categories or time buckets.
The data communicates parts of a total.
alternatives.reason: Donut provides the part-to-whole and center-summary treatment.

accessibility: Provide a visible legend or adjacent textual summary when exact series values are important; the chart image name alone is not a complete data table.
Do not rely on series color alone to communicate meaning.
A future point tooltip must supplement rather than replace accessible series and value context.
states: The chart is currently static and does not own hover, selection, loading, or empty-state policy.
Point markers may be shown or hidden without changing the underlying series meaning.
Authored series colors override the shared categorical palette.
responsiveBehavior: The owner supplies dimensions and updates them when its layout changes.
Category positions are always equally spaced, even when labels represent time.
Reduce or pre-format dense labels before passing them; the chart renders authored labels without a continuous time scale.

customElements: Assign series and categories as JavaScript properties and keep every series aligned to the category count.
react: Pass stable series and category arrays with dimensions derived from the containing layout.
angular: Bind series and categories as properties with dimensions derived from the containing layout.

categories.description: X-axis labels — must match each series' `data` length. Set as a JS property.

series.description: One or more series to plot. Set as a JS property (not an HTML attribute).

categories.description: X-axis labels — must match each series' `data` length. Set as a JS property.

series.description: One or more series to plot. Set as a JS property (not an HTML attribute).

consumption.complexPropertyNote: Assign these non-primitive values as JavaScript properties: categories, series.

peerDependencies.frameworks: Custom Elements; React 18/19 wrappers; Angular 19-22 standalone adapters.
